title: How to get a police certificate – Panama - Canada.ca
content:
## How to get a police certificate – Panama
If you need to give your fingerprints for a police certificate, this isn’t the same as giving your biometrics (fingerprints and photo) for an application.

## Name of the document you need
Record Policivo de Uso Internacional

## Police certificate and IRCC requirements
You must provide a police certificate when you apply for:
- some temporary residence programs
Check your application package or instructions for details.

### If you are a citizen of Panama

#### If you live in Panama
Apply in person or send a representative to apply for you at the Departamento de Investigación Judicial (see Contact information).

#### If you live outside of Panama
Apply in person at the General Consulate of Panama or send a representative to apply for you at the Departamento de Investigación Judicial in Panama (see Contact information).

### If you are not a citizen of Panama

#### If you live in Panama
Apply in person or send a representative to apply for you at the Departamento de Investigación Judicial (see Contact information).

#### If you live outside Panama
Apply in person or send a representative to apply for you at the Departamento de Investigación Judicial (see Contact information).

### Information and documents you need
- Your identification document ( Cédula ) or passport (or a photocopy if a representative is applying for you)
- If a representative applies for you, they must have notarized authorization.
- Forms are to be completed at the Dirección de Investigación Judicial or at the General Consulate of Panama on the day of the request.
- You must specify the certificate is for “ Uso Internacional ”
- Fiscal stamps, purchased from Banco Nacional , are required to issue the certificate. For more information on fiscal stamps, contact the Departamento de Autenticación y Legislación (see Contact information and address below).
- If you are not a citizen of Panama, the information and documents you need are listed on the Departamento de Autenticación y Legislación website (see Contact information and address below).
Dirección de Investigación Judicial (available in Spanish only) DIJ Ancón (Front of the Supply Market) Telephone: (507) 512-2222 / (507) 512-2210 / (507) 512-2246
DIJ San Miguelito (Roosevelt) Telephone: (507) 512-2509 / (507) 512-2510 / (507) 512-2511
DIJ La Chorrera Phones: (507) 244-5888 / (507) 244-1682
Departamento de Autenticación y Legalización (available in Spanish only) Avenida Ricardo J. Alfaro, Edificio Plaza Edison Local # 15 Panama Telephone: (507) 511-4045/4046 Fax: (507) 511-4061
